data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: UI elements are designed to be easily accessible to reduce interaction time.
data.technical_spec: Larger clickable areas for buttons placed within easy reach.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Limiting choices presented to users to speed up decision-making.
data.technical_spec: Organized navigation and minimalistic approach in software design.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: The design accommodates user's ability to process information in chunks.
data.technical_spec: Information is grouped in small, digestible parts.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: User experiences are made consistent across different Adobe applications.
data.technical_spec: Unified interface design language across product suites.

data.title: Tesler’s Law
data.description: Complexity is kept under control to maintain user efficiency.
data.technical_spec: Automation of repetitive tasks and simplifying advanced features.
